checked_queries: DkmsKeyRequest encrypted_secrets secret_signature user_public_key eight fields
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: DkmsKeyRequest no longer has the five-field no-secrets ABI; the local codec requires eight fields including encrypted_secrets, secret_signature, and user_public_key.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-precompile-codecs/src/dkms_key.rs decodes eight ABI tokens and exposes base ExecutorRequest fields plus owner/key fields.
No audited repo contains a five-token DKMS codec for the current port.

notes: This Atlas record conflicts with the newer eight-field DKMS invariant in this same batch.
rationale: The new codec documents that DKMS does not use encrypted_secrets, secret_signature, or user_public_key and provides dedicated ABI encode/decode logic for its simpler key derivation request. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: DkmsKeyRequest must encode and decode ABI fields [executor address, owner address, key_index uint256 as u64, key_format uint8, ttl uint256 as u64], and must fail decoding when the field count or token types are wrong or key_format does not fit in u8.
suggested_falsification_tests: Round-trip a DkmsKeyRequest through to_abi/from_abi and assert every field is preserved; then feed malformed ABI data, wrong token types, and a key_format uint256 above 255 and assert from_abi returns CodecError.
summary: The five-field DKMS ABI is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Round-trip a DkmsKeyRequest through to_abi/from_abi and assert every field is preserved; then feed malformed ABI data, wrong token types, and a key_format uint256 above 255 and assert from_abi returns CodecError.
title: DKMS request ABI has five fields and no secrets
